beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, stretch routine at home
beats.reasoning: Full energy start to Monday; Divya's fitness obsession means she begins with a light stretch and coffee before heading out. Ballard neighborhood fits her outdoorsy Seattle vibe.

beats.activity: Run trail run at Discovery Park before work
beats.reasoning: Sunny, 74F high—perfect weather for Divya's beloved trail running. Discovery Park is minutes from home; she prioritizes morning fitness before heading to Tukwila.

beats.activity: Return home, shower, change into work clothes
beats.reasoning: Quick post-run refresh; Divya showers and preps for commute to Tukwila workplace.
Light Rail closure between Westlake and U-District (9am–3pm) doesn't affect Ballard→Tukwila route; drive is standard ~45 min in light Monday traffic.

beats.activity: Event planning work: vendor calls, contract review, client consultations
beats.reasoning: Core work block; Divya handles wedding and event logistics. Ends early to catch the 11am FIFA match—she's curious about international sports and social atmosphere.

beats.activity: Lunch and watch USA vs Wales FIFA match at a downtown sports bar
beats.reasoning: Divya's curiosity and love of Seattle social spots draw her downtown for the 11am kickoff (11am PDT = noon local); she catches the match with a burger and energy drink, back by 1pm.

beats.activity: Return to Tukwila office to finish workday
beats.reasoning: Drive back to wrap up afternoon tasks; ~40 min commute accounts for post-lunch traffic on I-5.

beats.activity: Finish workday: final emails, timeline planning for upcoming weddings
beats.reasoning: Afternoon push to complete Monday checklist; event planning requires meticulous follow-up before end of business.
Evening commute; ~45 min from Tukwila to Ballard in moderate traffic.

beats.activity: Prep fly-fishing gear and review transit maps from recent trip
beats.reasoning: Divya unwinds with her hobbies—organizing fly-fishing equipment and curating her transit map collection feeds her curiosity; a quiet Monday evening suits her preference for close friends over crowds.

beats.activity: Light dinner: salad and grilled salmon
beats.reasoning: Healthy, protein-rich meal aligns with her fitness focus; eaten at home to stay grounded after a full work day.

beats.activity: Read, journal about upcoming weddings, early wind-down
beats.reasoning: Wind-down activity to prepare for sleep; Divya reflects on the day and plans mentally for the week ahead.
Solid 7.5-hour sleep after an active, full day; spans overnight as required.

diary:
Started the day right with coffee and a quick stretch, then hit Discovery Park for a trail run before the heat set in. The morning air was perfect—exactly what I needed to clear my head before a full Monday.

Work was steady: vendor calls, contract reviews, the usual cascade of details that keep weddings from falling apart. Around noon I ducked out to Kremwerk downtown to catch the USA-Wales match with a beer and some wings. The place was already buzzing even though it was early. Felt good to step away for a bit.

Back to the office to finish up timelines for two June ceremonies—one outdoor, one at a venue near Pike Place. By evening I was home in Ballard, tinkering with my fly-fishing gear and reviewing some trail maps from a recent trip. Made salmon for dinner, journaled a bit about the weddings rolling in. Early to bed tonight; June's going to be relentless.
thought_process: The Light Rail closure didn't affect me since I drive to Tukwila anyway, but I was aware of it—those transit disruptions always ripple through the city. The World Cup match felt like a natural break point; I could've skipped it, but Monday needed a little breathing room, and the downtown energy was infectious. I chose to duck out rather than eat at my desk because my mind was getting fuzzy from back-to-back calls. The rest of the day felt grounded—work, home, routine. By evening I was in reflection mode, thinking about the wedding season ahead and wanting to mentally reset with something tactile like prepping gear. The sunny weather kept my mood light throughout.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
